questions.question: In a box-and-whisker plot, what does the line inside the box represent?
What is the primary purpose of a box-and-whisker plot?

options.A: To display the mean of a dataset
options.B: To show the range of a dataset
options.C: To visualize the distribution of a dataset

questions.question: A scientist records the temperature of a planet in degrees Celsius: $-10, 0, 5, 10, 15, 20, 25$.
The diameter of a black hole is measured in kilometers: $1000, 1200, 1500, 1800, 2000, 2500$.
What is the interquartile range (IQR) of the dataset $2, 4, 6, 8, 10$?
The following box-and-whisker plot represents the scores of a physics exam: $Q_1 = 60, Q_3 = 80$.
A box-and-whisker plot shows the following values: $Min = 10, Q_1 = 20, Median = 30, Q_3 = 40, Max = 50$. What is the range of the data?
Which of the following is an outlier in the dataset $1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 100$?

Create a box-and-whisker plot to represent the following dataset of planetary distances from the sun in millions of kilometers: $50, 70, 100, 120, 150, 200, 250$. Identify the quartiles and interquartile range.

The speed of a spacecraft is recorded in kilometers per hour: $50, 60, 70, 80, 90, 100$. Determine the median and interquartile range. Show your calculations and provide a brief explanation of your results.

teacher_rules: Tip 1: Remind students to label their diagrams and plots clearly.
Tip 2: Encourage students to use a ruler to draw their box-and-whisker plots.
Tip 3: Emphasize the importance of accurately identifying quartiles and the interquartile range.
